verses.text: You who 'were dead in trespasses and sins,' walking after the world and the flesh. Dead in sin. By nature all are spiritually dead.
You who 'were dead in trespasses and sins,' walking after the world and the flesh. Dead in sin. By nature all are spiritually dead.
You who 'were dead in trespasses and sins,' walking after the world and the flesh. Dead in sin. By nature all are spiritually dead.
'God, who is rich in mercy... hath quickened us together with Christ... by grace ye are saved.' Made alive. Mercy raises the dead soul.
'God, who is rich in mercy... hath quickened us together with Christ... by grace ye are saved.' Made alive. Mercy raises the dead soul.
He raised us up 'and made us sit together in heavenly places,' to show His grace. Exalted. We are lifted with Christ to glory.
He raised us up 'and made us sit together in heavenly places,' to show His grace. Exalted. We are lifted with Christ to glory.
'By grace are ye saved through faith... it is the gift of God: not of works, lest any man should boast.' Free salvation. Grace, not works, saves.
'By grace are ye saved through faith... it is the gift of God: not of works, lest any man should boast.' Free salvation. Grace, not works, saves.
We are 'his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus unto good works.' His handiwork. Saved to do the good He prepared.
Ye Gentiles, once 'afar off, are made nigh by the blood of Christ.' Brought near. The blood reconciles the outsider.
Ye Gentiles, once 'afar off, are made nigh by the blood of Christ.' Brought near. The blood reconciles the outsider.
Ye Gentiles, once 'afar off, are made nigh by the blood of Christ.' Brought near. The blood reconciles the outsider.
'He is our peace, who hath made both one,' breaking 'the middle wall of partition.' One people. Christ unites Jew and Gentile in Himself.
'He is our peace, who hath made both one,' breaking 'the middle wall of partition.' One people. Christ unites Jew and Gentile in Himself.
'He is our peace, who hath made both one,' breaking 'the middle wall of partition.' One people. Christ unites Jew and Gentile in Himself.
He preached peace; 'through him we both have access by one Spirit unto the Father.' Access. All come to God through Christ.
He preached peace; 'through him we both have access by one Spirit unto the Father.' Access. All come to God through Christ.
Ye are 'fellowcitizens with the saints,' built on the apostles, 'Jesus Christ himself being the chief corner stone.' God's building. The church is His holy temple.
Ye are 'fellowcitizens with the saints,' built on the apostles, 'Jesus Christ himself being the chief corner stone.' God's building. The church is His holy temple.
Ye are 'fellowcitizens with the saints,' built on the apostles, 'Jesus Christ himself being the chief corner stone.' God's building. The church is His holy temple.
